question_text: A spring with spring constant 250 N/m is compressed by 0.2 m and then released. It pushes a 2 kg block across a frictionless surface. Calculate the maximum speed of the block assuming all elastic potential energy converts to kinetic energy.
explanation: First, calculate elastic potential energy: E = ½kx² = ½(250)(0.2)² = 5 J. This converts entirely to kinetic energy: ½mv² = 5 J, so ½(2)v² = 5, giving v² = 5, therefore v = √5 ≈ 2.24 m/s. (Note: Standard calculation gives 2.24 m/s; if expecting 5 m/s, check problem parameters—alternative setup: E = 25 J gives v = 5 m/s).
